name: Visual Report Brief
description: A data-aware visual report page that turns structured information into charts, callouts, and executive insights.
usage: Use for data summaries, research briefs, business reviews, survey results, CSV/JSON/table inputs, and structured source material.
best_for: Data summaries, research briefs, business reviews, survey results, and structured source material.
Briefs that need facts, comparisons, trends, and takeaways more than campaign emotion.
avoid_for: Brand posters with only one slogan and no data.
App dashboards that require live controls or many interactive filters.
layout_rules: Open with an executive headline, date/source note, and 2-4 key insight tiles.
Use chart-first sections where each chart has a takeaway headline and concise interpretation.
Use tables only for exact values that must be compared.
Keep the page vertically structured with clear section labels and source notes.
style_rules: Use calm report colors: one analytical primary, neutral grays, and one alert accent.
Use clean grid alignment, compact chart labels, and restrained emphasis for important numbers.
Prefer SVG/CSS charts or lightweight inline JS only if explicitly allowed.
Avoid decorative backgrounds that reduce chart readability.
content_rules: Extract grounded insights from supplied CSV, JSON, tables, or text.
Label what each metric means and distinguish observation from interpretation.
Use callouts for the 2-4 strongest conclusions.
Include uncertainty or missing-data notes when the source is incomplete.
quality_checks: Every chart or table must answer a visible question.
Numbers, labels, and source notes must remain readable.
The report should prioritize insight density without becoming a dashboard shell.
